_meta.title: OSPF/OSPFv3/IS-IS (Intermediate System to Intermediate System) topology online visualization for network engineers
_meta.description: Topolograph is a free tool for visualization of OSPF/IS-IS (Intermediate System to Intermediate System) topology based on OSPF/IS-IS LSDB output from Arista, Cisco, Juniper, Nokia, Mikrotik, Huawei devices and make a forecast of network reaction on outages
_meta.meta_keywords: topolograph, OSPF topology, OSPF visualization, OSPF graph, OSPF BGP-LS topology, IS-IS BGP-LS topology, Intermediate System to Intermediate System visualization, IS-IS BGP-LS graph
_meta.og_title: OSPF/OSPFv3/IS-IS topology online visualization for network engineers
_meta.og_description: Topolograph is a free tool for visualization of OSPF/IS-IS (Intermediate System to Intermediate System) topology based on OSPF/IS-IS LSDB output from Arista, Cisco, Juniper, Nokia, Mikrotik, Huawei devices and make a forecast of network reaction on outages. BGP-LS for OSPF is supported since version 2.59.

hero_lead_before: This is a OSPF/IS-IS visualization and analytics tool for network engineers which is aimed at working with OSPF/IS-IS network
banner_v2533: v2.53.3 LLM Friendly! Topolograph now includes built-in MCP (Model Context Protocol) on top of API for seamless integration with Large Language Models and AI agents. Enable intelligent network analysis and automation!
banner_v254: v2.54 IS-IS BGP-LS Topolograph now supports IS-IS BGP-LS topology visualization and analysis!
banner_v258: v2.58 Traffic Engineering (TE) link attributes onboarded!
banner_v259: v2.59 OSPF BGP-LS Topolograph now supports OSPF BGP-LS topology visualization and analysis!
how_start_heading: How to start working with your OSPF/IS-IS network graph offline?
how_start_li1_pre: Find your device's vendor in the
how_start_li1_post: and save the command's outputs from one of your routers as a single TXT file.
how_start_li2: Upload it to the Topolograph (here or in docker's version) via UI or API.
how_start_no_login: No logins and passwords are needed to provide!
blockquote_intro: Once you get your network graph you can:
feat_simulate_link: Simulate a link outage and discover backup paths or backup of backup paths...
feat_simulate_router: Simulate a router shutdown. Look at traffic flow around the failed router
feat_loaded_nodes: Find the most loaded nodes, edges, fault tolerant nodes.
feat_focus: Browse your network with `Focus on the Node` option
feat_compare: Compare the network state at different times
feat_te: Traffic Engineering (TE) link attributes for IS-IS and OSPF (FRR opaque-area) - filter and analyze links by bandwidth, TE metric, and admin group
demo_img_alt: Build OSPF shortest paths in Topolograph. Get Backup paths in Topolograph.
card_demo_text: Test topolograph's functionality right now! A test graph with 13 hosts has already pre-uploaded for everyone.
card_viz_p1: 1. Save OSPF LSDB as txt file or use Topolograph SDK
card_analysis_p2: 2. Find network reaction on a failure
card_reports_text: Reports can help to discover undirected links or asymmetric paths is your topology
sec_demo_text1: In order to get familiar with the principles of calculating backup paths in the event of a link or node falling, you can play with
sec_demo_text2: Once you build SPT and check Backup paths, see network reaction on link or network shutdown etc!
sec_upload_heading_pre: Get your OSPF topology visual on the
sec_upload_intro: Save an output from your router (i.e. Cisco):
sec_upload_sdk_pre: If you have a multiarea network or need to collect LSDB output from multiple devices, use the
sec_upload_sdk_post: to fetch LSDB output from multiple devices/areas. The SDK provides a unified interface for collecting OSPF LSDB data from various network devices. For not-registered users only one topology is saved. For registered users this limit is increased upto 5 topologies.
sec_spt_heading: Check how the shortest path goes.
sec_spt_p1: Traceroute or mtr is not helpful when a packet goes through a firewall. The firewall won't appear in a trace. In order to check the actual path through all network devices - build the shortest path using several ways:
sec_spt_p2: In order to see a backup path - just press on a colored spt edge link. The algorithm will exclude this link and build a backup path.
sec_nfp_heading: What will be if we shutdown our Core switch or... one of our border router?)
sec_nfp_p1: You can find the network reaction in NetworkReactionOnFailure tab. Just choose your node (network device) and simulate shutdown from right-clicked menu. The algorithm will exclude this node from the topology and show you consequences of your test.
sec_cost_heading: Change OSPF cost on a link on the fly.
sec_cost_p1: Right-click on an edge and set a new OSPF cost. Topolograph shows the traffic change flow - links with a decreased and increased amount of traffic. If we set OSPF cost 9 between Node A and Node B - traffic will be redirected to a link via Node C and Node D. This feature is going to be used when you want to adjust OSPF cost and would like to see the output of your changes.
sec_asym_heading: Report. Asymmetric paths in OSPF domains.
sec_asym_p1: When different costs are configured on different links - asymmetric paths could be in the network. The incoming path from W to F is going via C-D, but the outgoing path is via B-A. Paths can go via different ISPs and come with different delays and, probably, losses. The report is aimed at discovering such cases in order to eliminate it.
sec_interarea_heading: Report. Backup paths via 3d party locations.
sec_interarea_intro: This report checks that if two locations are directly connected, backup paths should be only between two locations and do not go via third location as transit.
sec_interarea_p1a: Before running this report - it's needed to create
sec_interarea_p2: For example, there are two locations in France: the main site (EU_FRA) and the remote site (EU_FRA1). The same schema with offices in Italy. The main offices in France and Italy are connected to each other and have main (OSPF cost 10) and backup (OSPF cost 20) link.
sec_interarea_p3: Remote offices have the main link (OSPF cost 1) to their main offices and backup link (OSPF cost 10) to the foreign office.
sec_interarea_p4: If the main link between main offices goes down - the backup path goes via remote offices!
sec_heatmap_p1: The topolograph knows what networks are advertised by nodes. When the network is terminated on both routers, using VRRP, both nodes advertise the network. The node is marked by red if it has a lot of unbackuped networks, and vise versa.
sec_privacy_heading: Privacy. Keep your network inside your organization.
sec_privacy_p1a: Run your local copy of Topolograph inside your on-premises network using the docker compose file
sec_llm_p1: Topolograph now supports Large Language Models (LLMs) through built-in MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. AI agents can directly query network topologies, analyze OSPF/IS-IS events, calculate paths, and perform intelligent network analysis. This enables automated network troubleshooting, predictive analysis, and intelligent network operations.
commands_ospf_lsdb_intro: Save the output from min two commands (for getting LSA1 and LSA2) into a file with .txt or .log extension and upload it to Topolograph.
commands_ospfv3_lsdb_intro: OSPFv3 (RFC 5340) LSDB topology visualization. Save the output from the command into a file with .txt or .log extension and upload it to Topolograph.
commands_isis_lsdb_intro: Save the output from the command into a file with .txt or .log extension and upload it to Topolograph.
commands_isis_frr_hostname_note: *FRR mixes LSPIDs and dynamic hostnames in IS-IS LSDB, that's why building IS-IS topology without hostnames is supported only. "no hostname dynamic" disables dynamic hostnames in the local IS-IS LSDB.
